long_range_ising_dicke.dtype_rule: Float64 safe only when spin_coupling_dir, spin_field_dir, and boson_coupling_dir are all in {X, Z}.
long_range_ising_dicke.hamiltonian: H = J * sum_{i<j} sigma^spin_coupling_dir_i * sigma^spin_coupling_dir_j / |i-j|^alpha + h * sum_i sigma^spin_field_dir_i + omega * Bn + g * (a + adag) * sum_i sigma^boson_coupling_dir_i
long_range_ising_dicke.description: Power-law decaying Ising spin chain coupled to a single bosonic cavity mode. Extends Ising-Dicke by replacing nearest-neighbor spin coupling with exact long-range power-law interactions. ED computes the power-law sum exactly with no approximation.
long_range_ising_dicke.note: n_exp and N_spins (model-level) are required for TN only. Must be OMITTED from ED configs.

spin_coupling_dir.dtype_constraint: If Y, dtype must be ComplexF64.
spin_coupling_dir.description: Spin operator direction for long-range spin-spin coupling.

g.description: Spin-boson coupling strength for g*(a+adag)*sum_i sigma_i.

omega.description: Boson cavity mode frequency. Coefficient of Bn.

boson_coupling_dir.dtype_constraint: If Y, dtype must be ComplexF64.
boson_coupling_dir.description: Collective spin operator direction in the spin-boson coupling.

alpha.description: Power-law decay exponent. alpha=0 is all-to-all equal coupling. alpha->inf recovers nearest-neighbor.

spin_field_dir.dtype_constraint: If Y, dtype must be ComplexF64.
spin_field_dir.description: Spin operator direction for the field term.

h.description: Spin field strength applied uniformly to all spin sites.

long_range_ising.dtype_rule: Float64 safe only when coupling_dir and field_dir are both in {X, Z}.
long_range_ising.hamiltonian: H = J * sum_{i<j} sigma^coupling_dir_i * sigma^coupling_dir_j / |i-j|^alpha + h * sum_i sigma^field_dir_i
long_range_ising.description: Power-law decaying all-to-all Ising interactions with a field. Coupling decays as 1/|i-j|^alpha. ED computes this EXACTLY — a key advantage over TN methods which require exponential approximations.
long_range_ising.note: n_exp and N (model-level) are required for TN only to approximate power-law via sum-of-exponentials FSM. Must be OMITTED from ED configs.

J.description: Overall coupling strength. J<0 ferromagnetic, J>0 antiferromagnetic.

coupling_dir.dtype_constraint: If Y, dtype must be ComplexF64.
coupling_dir.description: Spin operator direction for long-range coupling.

alpha.description: Power-law decay exponent. alpha=0 is infinite-range equal coupling. alpha=1 is Coulomb-like. alpha=2 is dipolar. alpha->inf recovers nearest-neighbor.

field_dir.dtype_constraint: If Y, dtype must be ComplexF64.
field_dir.description: Spin operator direction for the field term.

h.description: External field strength applied to all sites.

transverse_field_ising.dtype_rule: Float64 is safe only when coupling_dir and field_dir are both in {X, Z}. Use ComplexF64 if either is Y.
transverse_field_ising.hamiltonian: H = J * sum_i sigma^coupling_dir_i * sigma^coupling_dir_{i+1} + h * sum_i sigma^field_dir_i
transverse_field_ising.description: Nearest-neighbor Ising model with a transverse (or longitudinal) field. The simplest model exhibiting a quantum phase transition at |J/h| = 1 in the thermodynamic limit.

J.description: Nearest-neighbor coupling strength. J < 0 is ferromagnetic. J > 0 is antiferromagnetic.

coupling_dir.dtype_constraint: If Y is chosen, system dtype must be ComplexF64.
coupling_dir.description: Spin operator direction for the two-site nearest-neighbor coupling term. Conventionally Z for Ising models.

field_dir.dtype_constraint: If Y is chosen, system dtype must be ComplexF64.
field_dir.description: Spin operator direction for the single-site field term. Must differ from coupling_dir for a transverse field. Conventionally X for transverse field Ising.

h.description: External field strength. At the critical point |h/J| = 1 (for Z coupling, X field) the system undergoes a quantum phase transition.

ising_dicke.dtype_rule: Float64 safe only when spin_coupling_dir, spin_field_dir, and boson_coupling_dir are all in {X, Z}.
ising_dicke.hamiltonian: H = J * sum_i sigma^spin_coupling_dir_i * sigma^spin_coupling_dir_{i+1} + h * sum_i sigma^spin_field_dir_i + omega * Bn + g * (a + adag) * sum_i sigma^boson_coupling_dir_i
ising_dicke.description: Nearest-neighbor Ising spin chain coupled to a single bosonic cavity mode (Dicke model). Combines Ising spin-spin interactions and a spin field with a cavity frequency term and collective spin-boson coupling.

spin_coupling_dir.dtype_constraint: If Y, dtype must be ComplexF64.
spin_coupling_dir.description: Spin operator direction for nearest-neighbor spin-spin coupling.

J.description: Nearest-neighbor spin-spin coupling. J<0 ferromagnetic, J>0 antiferromagnetic.

g.description: Spin-boson coupling strength. Superradiant phase transition near g_c = sqrt(omega*|J|)/2 in the thermodynamic limit.

omega.description: Boson cavity mode frequency. Coefficient of the Bn number operator.

boson_coupling_dir.dtype_constraint: If Y, dtype must be ComplexF64.
boson_coupling_dir.description: Collective spin operator direction in the spin-boson coupling g*(a+adag)*sum_i sigma^dir_i. Typically X for transverse Dicke coupling.

spin_field_dir.dtype_constraint: If Y, dtype must be ComplexF64.
spin_field_dir.description: Spin operator direction for the spin field term.

h.description: Spin field strength applied uniformly to all spin sites.

heisenberg.dtype_rule: Float64 is safe only when Jy=0 and hy=0. Any nonzero Jy or hy requires ComplexF64.
heisenberg.hamiltonian: H = sum_i (Jx*Sx_i*Sx_{i+1} + Jy*Sy_i*Sy_{i+1} + Jz*Sz_i*Sz_{i+1}) + sum_i (hx*Sx_i + hy*Sy_i + hz*Sz_i)
heisenberg.description: Anisotropic Heisenberg spin chain with nearest-neighbor XXZ couplings and optional uniform magnetic field in all three directions. Reduces to isotropic Heisenberg when Jx=Jy=Jz and all fields zero.

Jy.description: YY nearest-neighbor coupling. Requires ComplexF64 if nonzero.

Jz.description: ZZ nearest-neighbor coupling. Jz=0 gives XX model.

hy.description: Uniform field in Y direction. Requires ComplexF64 if nonzero.

hz.description: Uniform field in Z direction on all sites.

hx.description: Uniform field in X direction on all sites.

user_models.description: User-registered named custom models. Each entry is a named model built via the custom model term builder in the GUI and saved for reuse. The engine looks up models here when the name is not found in prebuilt_models or custom_models. Entries are added automatically via POST /api/registry/models.

custom_spinboson.name: custom_spinboson

dicke_custom.description: Full Dicke model: ZZ spin coupling + Z field + cavity frequency + transverse Dicke coupling.

jaynes_cummings_chain.description: Jaynes-Cummings spin-boson model: cavity coupled to spin chain via rotating wave approximation.

terms.spin_terms: <array of EDField or EDCoupling objects — same format as custom_spin>

boson_terms.description: Pure boson terms acting only on the cavity mode. Each term is a single boson operator multiplied by a strength coefficient. Use Bn for the cavity frequency (omega * Bn = omega * b†b). Use a and adag for classical drive terms.

strength.description: Coefficient multiplying the boson operator. For Bn this is the cavity frequency omega.

spinboson_terms.note: Only single-site spin operators are supported (field-type, not two-site). Two-site spin interactions coupled to the boson must be built as separate spin_terms and boson_terms.
spinboson_terms.description: Spin-boson coupling terms of the form: strength * boson_op * sum_i spin_op_i. Each term couples one boson operator to the same spin operator applied collectively to all spin sites. For the standard Dicke coupling g*(a+adag)*sum_i sigma^x_i, use two terms: one with boson_op=a and one with boson_op=adag. For Jaynes-Cummings g*(a*Sp + adag*Sm), use boson_op=a with spin_op=Sp and boson_op=adag with spin_op=Sm.

spin_op.description: Single-site spin operator applied collectively to all spin sites (summed over all N_spins spins).

boson_op.description: Boson operator for this coupling term.

spin_terms.format: Array of EDField or EDCoupling objects. See custom_spin term_types for full specification.
spin_terms.note: Site indices in patterns refer to spin sites only (1 to N_spins). The boson site is handled separately.
spin_terms.description: Pure spin terms acting only on the spin sites. Identical format to custom_spin terms — supports EDField and EDCoupling with all six coupling patterns. These are automatically tensored with the boson identity on the boson site.

custom_spinboson.description: Assemble an arbitrary spin-boson Hamiltonian from three independent term categories: pure spin terms (EDField/EDCoupling on spin sites), pure boson terms (single boson operator on cavity), and spin-boson coupling terms (boson operator * collective spin operator). Backend parser: _parse_custom_spinboson_terms() in ed_models.jl.

custom_spin.name: custom_spin

frustrated_chain.description: J1-J2 frustrated chain: nearest-neighbor + next-nearest-neighbor ZZ couplings with transverse field.

jaynes_cummings_spin_only.description: Raising-lowering nearest-neighbor hopping: Sp_i Sm_{i+1} + Sm_i Sp_{i+1}. Requires ComplexF64.

xy_model.description: XY model: XX + YY nearest-neighbor couplings. Requires ComplexF64.

range.description: Site separation distance. Required for finite_range patterns.

alpha.description: Power-law decay exponent. Required for power_law pattern.

op1.description: Spin operator on site i (the left site).

allowed_values.all_to_all: Couples all pairs i<j with uniform strength J. Equivalent to power_law with alpha=0.
allowed_values.nearest_neighbor_periodic: Couples site i to i+1 for all i, including site N back to site 1. Periodic boundary conditions.
allowed_values.nearest_neighbor: Couples site i to i+1 for all i. Open boundary, no wrap. H += J * sum_i op1_i * op2_{i+1}.
allowed_values.power_law: Couples all pairs i<j with strength J/|i-j|^alpha. Requires 'alpha' field. Exact computation, no approximation.
allowed_values.finite_range_periodic: Couples site i to site (i+range) mod N. Periodic boundary. Requires 'range' field.
allowed_values.finite_range: Couples site i to site i+range for all valid i. Open boundary. Requires 'range' field.

pattern.description: Interaction pattern determining which pairs (i,j) are coupled and how.

op2.description: Spin operator on site j (the right site).

alpha.description: Power-law decay exponent. Required only for pattern=power_law. Coupling decays as 1/|i-j|^alpha.

range.description: Site separation distance. Required for finite_range and finite_range_periodic. range=1 is nearest-neighbor, range=2 is next-nearest-neighbor.

EDCoupling.description: Two-site coupling term: strength * sum_{(i,j)} coeff(i,j) * op1_i * op2_j. The pattern field determines which site pairs are coupled and with what spatial weight.

strength.description: Field strength coefficient. Contributes strength * sum_i op_i to H.

op.description: Spin operator applied to each site.

EDField.description: Single-site field term. Applies the same operator uniformly to every spin site: strength * sum_i op_i.

custom_spin.description: Assemble an arbitrary spin Hamiltonian term by term. Each term is either a single-site field (EDField) or a two-site coupling (EDCoupling) with a specified interaction pattern. The engine parses the terms array and builds the exact Hamiltonian matrix. Backend parser: _parse_custom_spin_terms() in ed_models.jl.

display_name.description: Human-readable label shown in the GUI model dropdown.

system_type.description: 'spin' or 'spinboson'. Determines which parser is called at runtime.

terms.description: For spin: array of EDField/EDCoupling objects (same as custom_spin params.terms). For spinboson: object with keys spin_terms, boson_terms, spinboson_terms (same as custom_spinboson params.terms).

description.description: User-provided physical description of the model.

frustrated_chain.description: J1-J2 model with next-nearest-neighbor frustration and transverse field

custom_dicke_variant.description: Long-range ZZ spin coupling + cavity + Dicke coupling

registration_schema.description: Schema for a user_models entry. When a custom model is registered via the GUI, this structure is saved to user_models.models.

description: Defines all available Hamiltonians — prebuilt models with fixed parameter specs and custom model grammars for assembling arbitrary Hamiltonians term by term. User-registered named models are stored in user_models.
